cot_prefix: Okay, let's try to figure out when Andrew could have gone to the coffee shop. Let me start by going through all the given information step by step. First, Andrew woke up at 8am. The coffee shop was closed after 8pm. So, the earliest he could have gone is after 8am, and the latest would be before 8pm. Now, let's look at the people who saw Andrew at different times and places: 1. Elizabeth saw Andrew fixing their computer at the electronic store from 11am to 3pm. 2. Jennifer saw Andrew buying a bike at the bike shop from 3pm to 4pm. 3. Anthony saw Andrew buying cookies at a bakery from 4pm to 6pm. 4. Betty saw Andrew watching a movie at the theater from 6pm to 8pm. Wait a second, so between 11am and 3pm, Andrew was at the electronic store. Then from 3pm to 4pm at the bike shop, 4pm to 6pm at the bakery, and 6pm to 8pm at the theater. The coffee shop was closed after 8pm, so he couldn't have gone after that. But the key here is that if someone saw Andrew at a place during those times, then he couldn't be at the coffee shop during those overlapping times. So the times when Andrew was seen elsewhere are: 11am-3pm (computer) 3pm-4pm (bike) 4pm-6pm (cookies) 6pm-8pm (movie) So the only time when Andrew wasn't seen doing something else is between when he woke up at 8am and when Elizabeth saw him at 11am. That would be 8am to 11am. Because from 11am onward, he was seen doing various activities until 8pm. So the possible time he could have gone to the coffee shop is between 8am and 11am. Let me check the options again. Options are: A) 3pm to 4pm – but he was at the bike shop then, so no. B) 6pm to 8pm – he was at the theater then. C) 8am to 11am – this is the only time not accounted for, except waking up. D) 11am to 3pm – he was at the electronic store then. Therefore, the answer should be option C: 8am to 11am.
